profile.name: Architecture Documentation Freeze
profile.description: Generates a deterministic Markdown architecture snapshot from the current OpenLAG graph.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle Evolution
artifacts.description: Contract-driven lifecycle evolution adding starter onboarding, freeze/export, rule contracts, profile packs, preview workflow, and self-documenting release evidence.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle Evolution

This version documents how OpenLAG evolves from the 0.4.0 baseline into the 0.5.0 contract-driven lifecycle model.

The root repository now acts as the primary OpenLAG project. Its lifecycle documentation is stored in `docs/*`, while `internal/dev-sandbox` remains a secondary internal dataset for compatibility checks and example validation.

metadata.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle Evolution
metadata.description: Contract-driven lifecycle evolution adding starter onboarding, freeze/export, rule contracts, profile packs, preview workflow, and self-documenting release evidence.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.4.0 Release Baseline
artifacts.description: Published baseline containing project-local artifact contracts, relation runtime payloads, richer lint report data, dynamic portal contract metadata, and init-all support.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.4.0 Release Baseline

This version represents the audited stable baseline before the 0.5.0 lifecycle evolution.

In this baseline, the repository-root `docs/*` dataset was the observable OpenLAG project used to generate the architecture graph and release portal evidence.

metadata.title: OpenLAG 0.4.0 Release Baseline
metadata.description: Published baseline containing project-local artifact contracts, relation runtime payloads, richer lint report data, dynamic portal contract metadata, and init-all support.

artifacts.title: Freeze and Export Evidence
artifacts.description: OpenLAG can freeze architecture documentation into deterministic markdown, JSON, HTML, and PDF evidence outputs.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Freeze and Export Evidence

OpenLAG 0.5.0 introduces release evidence generation through the `freeze` workflow and export profiles.

This makes lifecycle documentation portable across review, audit, and release contexts.

metadata.title: Freeze and Export Evidence
metadata.description: OpenLAG can freeze architecture documentation into deterministic markdown, JSON, HTML, and PDF evidence outputs.

artifacts.title: Preview Workflow
artifacts.description: OpenLAG can generate and build a static portal for inspecting architecture graph outputs.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Preview Workflow

The preview workflow turns project documentation into graph data and a static portal.

For OpenLAG 0.5.0, the primary preview is generated from repository-root `docs/*`; sandbox previews remain secondary.

metadata.title: Preview Workflow
metadata.description: OpenLAG can generate and build a static portal for inspecting architecture graph outputs.

artifacts.title: Profile Packs
artifacts.description: OpenLAG 0.5.0 packages reusable architecture, governance, starter, and domain profiles.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Profile Packs

Profile packs make OpenLAG usable across different documentation domains without forcing every project to hand-author the same contracts.

The root project uses the core contracts as its own lifecycle vocabulary and keeps the dev sandbox as a separate validation dataset.

metadata.title: Profile Packs
metadata.description: OpenLAG 0.5.0 packages reusable architecture, governance, starter, and domain profiles.

artifacts.title: Rule Contracts
artifacts.description: OpenLAG supports project-local rule contracts that make governance and validation behavior explicit.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Rule Contracts

Rule contracts allow OpenLAG projects to define validation behavior alongside artifacts and relations.

For the self-documenting repository, rule contracts are activated under `docs/contracts/rules`.

metadata.title: Rule Contracts
metadata.description: OpenLAG supports project-local rule contracts that make governance and validation behavior explicit.

artifacts.title: Starter Mode
artifacts.description: OpenLAG provides starter-oriented onboarding contracts and templates for lightweight project adoption.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Starter Mode

Starter mode lowers the cost of adopting OpenLAG by providing a compact artifact and relation vocabulary.

It complements the full core profile used by this repository for release lifecycle documentation.

metadata.title: Starter Mode
metadata.description: OpenLAG provides starter-oriented onboarding contracts and templates for lightweight project adoption.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Contract-Driven Evolution
artifacts.description: Documents the evolution from OpenLAG 0.4.0 to 0.5.0 using OpenLAG artifacts and release evidence.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.5.0 Contract-Driven Evolution

This change captures the architectural and documentary evolution between the 0.4.0 release baseline and the 0.5.0 release line.

The change reclassifies the repository root as the real self-documenting OpenLAG project and moves the previous example dataset boundary to `internal/dev-sandbox`.

metadata.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Contract-Driven Evolution
metadata.description: Documents the evolution from OpenLAG 0.4.0 to 0.5.0 using OpenLAG artifacts and release evidence.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Validation
artifacts.description: Validates the 0.5.0 lifecycle features through root generation, release checks, package dry run, and freeze outputs.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Validation

This test case covers the final validation path for OpenLAG 0.5.0:

- `npm.cmd run check`
- `node --import tsx scripts/cli/openlag.ts check --profile develop`
- `node --import tsx scripts/cli/openlag.ts check --profile release --strict`
- `node bin/openlag.js --version`
- `npm.cmd pack --dry-run`
- root-level generate, build, and freeze evidence

metadata.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Validation
metadata.description: Validates the 0.5.0 lifecycle features through root generation, release checks, package dry run, and freeze outputs.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG Self Lifecycle Documentation
artifacts.description: Documents the boundary between the root self-documenting project and the internal dev sandbox.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG Self Lifecycle Documentation

The OpenLAG repository root is the primary OpenLAG project for lifecycle documentation.

Root-level `docs/*` captures versions, changes, features, validation evidence, and governance notes for OpenLAG itself.

`internal/dev-sandbox/docs` remains an internal example and regression dataset. It can be generated and built after root validation, but it does not replace the root lifecycle graph.

metadata.title: OpenLAG Self Lifecycle Documentation
metadata.description: Documents the boundary between the root self-documenting project and the internal dev sandbox.
